steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The graph of $y = f(x)$ passes through $(0, 3)$, $(9, 0)$, and $(24, 0)$. The question asks which statement cannot be true — so look for the one that directly contradicts a known point.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$f(24) < 0$': The point $(24, 0)$ is on the graph, so $f(24) = 0$, not less than zero. ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(3) < 0$': The graph could dip below zero between $x = 0$ and $x = 9$ — no data rules this out. ✓
  • Choice C — '$f(3) > 0$': Similarly possible. ✓
  • Choice D — '$f(0) < 24$': $f(0) = 3$, and $3 < 24$, so this is true. ✓
That matches choice A — A is correct.
